name: Warm Salad of Clams and Cockles with Escarole, Rhubarb
recipeIngredient: 1 head escarole, cut into half-inch ribbons
2 red bell peppers, roasted, peeled, seeded and cut into one-quarter-inch julienne
recipeInstructions:
1. In a 12 to 14-inch sauté pan, heat the olive oil until it smokes.
2. Add garlic and rhubarb; cook until softened, about 4 to 5 minutes.
3. Add littleneck clams and white wine; bring to a boil.
4. Cover and simmer until all clams are open, approximately 6 to 8 minutes.
5. Meanwhile, prepare crostini by mixing roasted peppers, olive paste, and remaining oil.
- Divide the mixture among the toasted slices of baguette.
- Place three slices on the rims of each of four plates.
6. Add cockles to the clam pan; cover.
7. Cook until cockles open, about 1 minute.
8. Add vinegar and escarole; season with salt and pepper.
9. Stir through, then divide the escarole, clams, and cockles among the plates.
10. Serve.
Yield: 4 servings
